prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: condensed Fukui functions – electrophilic (f⁺), nucleophilic (f⁻), radial (f⁰ = ½(f⁺+f⁻)); most-reactive atomic sites identified. Units: electrons (dimensionless, Σf± ≈ 1).
• Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis (tier ‘standard’), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals).
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied acrolein coordinates (`acrolein.xyz`, 8 atoms); single-point vertical ionizations (N±1) on this fixed geometry (`DFT//B3LYP/def2-TZVP`). No additional optimization performed.
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no continuum solvation).
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (singlet); cation and anion states were doublets (charge ±1, mult 2).
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (backend: PySCF 2.x via chemkit-mcp).
• Convergence & caveats: all three SCFs (N, N−1, N+1) converged; integrity status ok, trustworthy = true.
  Warnings (verbatim):
  – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
  – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
  – common header present
• Live engine log streamed to `fukui_<timestamp>.out` in the working directory; result JSON saved at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpowi6dngy/live.json.

Headline numerical outcome
Most electrophilic site: atom 4 (C, β-carbon) with f⁺ = 0.64232102 (reported above under value).
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O) with f⁻ = 0.39388616.

These results were computed, not experimental.
